questions.question: Which is an RFC 1918 private network?
questions.explanation: 192.168.0.0/16 is one of the three RFC 1918 ranges.

correct.explanation: 192.168.0.0/16 is one of the three RFC 1918 ranges.

incorrect.explanation: 8.8.8.0/24 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 192 — not 8.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "8.8.8.0/24" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 203.0.113.0/24 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 192 — not 203.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "203.0.113.0/24" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 169.254.1.0/24 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 192 — not 169.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "169.254.1.0/24" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking 172.16.0.0/16 is the whole 172 private block — The private range is 172.16.0.0/12 (through 172.31.x.x)

incorrect.explanation: Public routable space reflects a common trap: Private addresses are secret/encrypted. Private means non-routable on the public Internet, not hidden from others on the same LAN.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Private addresses are secret/encrypted

incorrect.explanation: Multicast only misses what this question tests. 10.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Multicast only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: APIPA misses what this question tests. 10.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "APIPA" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking 172.16.0.0/16 is the whole 172 private block — The private range is 172.16.0.0/12 (through 172.31.x.x)

incorrect.explanation: No — public only misses what this question tests. 172.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "No — public only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Only if DHCP assigns it misses what this question tests. 172.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Only if DHCP assigns it" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Only on IPv6 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 172 — not 6.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Only on IPv6" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Thinking 172.16.0.0/16 is the whole 172 private block — The private range is 172.16.0.0/12 (through 172.31.x.x)

questions.question: A Windows PC shows IP 169.254.88.3. What is the most likely cause?
questions.explanation: 169.254.x.x is APIPA — self-assigned when DHCP fails.

correct.explanation: 169.254.x.x is APIPA — self-assigned when DHCP fails.

incorrect.explanation: Successful DHCP lease reflects a common trap: Treating 169.254.x.x as normal private DHCP address. 169.254 is APIPA — indicates DHCP failure, not RFC 1918.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Treating 169.254.x.x as normal private DHCP address

incorrect.explanation: Static public IP configured misses what this question tests. 169.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Static public IP configured" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: DNS cache flush misses what this question tests. 169.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "DNS cache flush" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Treating 169.254.x.x as normal private DHCP address — 169.254 is APIPA — indicates DHCP failure, not RFC 1918

questions.question: Why must private addresses use NAT to reach the Internet?
choices: They are not globally unique and are not routed publicly
questions.explanation: Private space is reused and non-routable on the Internet — NAT maps to a public address.

correct.explanation: Private space is reused and non-routable on the Internet — NAT maps to a public address.

incorrect.explanation: They are too short misses what this question tests. Private space is reused and non-routable on the Internet — NAT maps to a public address.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "They are too short" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: They are IPv6 only misses what this question tests. Private space is reused and non-routable on the Internet — NAT maps to a public address.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "They are IPv6 only" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Firewalls block all IPv4 misses what this question tests. Private space is reused and non-routable on the Internet — NAT maps to a public address.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Firewalls block all IPv4" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Mark inside/outside first. PAT = many inside hosts sharing one outside global with unique ports.

questions.question: Which address is public (Internet-routable)?
questions.explanation: 198.51.100.0/24 is documentation/public space — not RFC 1918 private.

correct.explanation: 198.51.100.0/24 is documentation/public space — not RFC 1918 private.

incorrect.explanation: 10.1.1.1 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 198 — not 10.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "10.1.1.1" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 172.16.1.1 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 198 — not 172.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "172.16.1.1" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 192.168.1.1 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 198 — not 192.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "192.168.1.1" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: This stem tests identify public — exam distractors swap similar terms; anchor on: 198.

questions.question: True or False: APIPA addresses are in the same RFC 1918 private ranges as 192.168.x.x.
questions.explanation: False — APIPA uses 169.254.0.0/16, separate from RFC 1918.

correct.explanation: False — APIPA uses 169.254.0.0/16, separate from RFC 1918.

incorrect.explanation: This statement is True, but the tested fact is: False — APIPA uses 169.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "True" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Treating 169.254.x.x as normal private DHCP address — 169.254 is APIPA — indicates DHCP failure, not RFC 1918

questions.question: Many PCs on 192.168.1.0/24 share one ISP public IP. Which technology enables this?
questions.explanation: NAT (often PAT) translates many private addresses to one or few public IPs.

correct.explanation: NAT (often PAT) translates many private addresses to one or few public IPs.

incorrect.explanation: STP is the wrong device role here. STP prevents Layer 2 loops — it does not provide inter-VLAN routing, MAC learning rules, or replace trunk configuration.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Choosing STP when the question asks for forwarding, routing, or VLAN data plane behavior

incorrect.explanation: VTP misses what this question tests. NAT (often PAT) translates many private addresses to one or few public IPs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing inside local, inside global, and outside addresses

incorrect.explanation: LLDP misses what this question tests. NAT (often PAT) translates many private addresses to one or few public IPs.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing inside local, inside global, and outside addresses

answerReview.examTip: Mark inside/outside first. PAT = many inside hosts sharing one outside global with unique ports.
